# All modules

MCP Kotlin SDK
==============

Kotlin SDK for the Model Context Protocol (MCP). This is a Kotlin Multiplatform library that helps you build MCP clients and servers that speak the same protocol and share the same types. The SDK focuses on clarity, small building blocks, and first‑class coroutine support.

Use the umbrella `kotlin-sdk` artifact when you want a single dependency that brings the core types plus both client and server toolkits. If you only need one side, depend on `kotlin-sdk-client` or `kotlin-sdk-server` directly.

Gradle (Kotlin DSL):

    dependencies {
        // Convenience bundle with everything you need to start
        implementation("io.modelcontextprotocol:kotlin-sdk:<version>")
    
        // Or pick modules explicitly
        implementation("io.modelcontextprotocol:kotlin-sdk-client:<version>")
        implementation("io.modelcontextprotocol:kotlin-sdk-server:<version>")
    }

Content copied to clipboard

Module kotlin-sdk-core
----------------------

Foundational, platform‑agnostic pieces:

*   Protocol data model and JSON serialization (kotlinx.serialization)
    
*   Request/response and notification types used by both sides of MCP
    
*   Coroutine‑friendly protocol engine and utilities
    
*   Transport abstractions shared by client and server
    

You typically do not use `core` directly in application code; it is pulled in by the client/server modules. Use it explicitly if you only need the protocol types or plan to implement a custom transport.

Module kotlin-sdk-client
------------------------

High‑level client API for connecting to an MCP server and invoking its tools, prompts, and resources. Ships with several transports:

*   WebSocketClientTransport – low latency, full‑duplex
    
*   SSEClientTransport – Server‑Sent Events over HTTP
    
*   StdioClientTransport – CLI‑friendly stdio bridge
    
*   StreamableHttpClientTransport – simple HTTP streaming
    

A minimal client:

    val client = Client(
        clientInfo = Implementation(name = "sample-client", version = "1.0.0")
    )
    
    client.connect(WebSocketClientTransport("ws://localhost:8080/mcp"))
    
    val tools = client.listTools()
    val result = client.callTool(
        name = "echo",
        arguments = mapOf("text" to "Hello, MCP!")
    )

Content copied to clipboard

Module kotlin-sdk-server
------------------------

Lightweight server toolkit for hosting MCP tools, prompts, and resources. It provides a small, composable API and ready‑to‑use transports:

*   StdioServerTransport – integrates well with CLIs and editors
    
*   SSE/WebSocket helpers for Ktor – easy HTTP deployment
    

Register tools and run over stdio:

    val server = Server(
        serverInfo = Implementation(name = "sample-server", version = "1.0.0"),
        options = ServerOptions(ServerCapabilities())
    )
    
    server.addTool(
        name = "echo",
        description = "Echoes the provided text"
    ) { request ->
        // Build and return a CallToolResult from request.arguments
        // (see CallToolResult and related types in kotlin-sdk-core)
        /* ... */
    }
    
    // Bridge the protocol over stdio
    val transport = StdioServerTransport(
        inputStream = kotlinx.io.files.Path("/dev/stdin").source(),
        outputStream = kotlinx.io.files.Path("/dev/stdout").sink()
    )
    // Start transport and wire it with the server using provided helpers in the SDK.

Content copied to clipboard

For HTTP deployments, use the Ktor extensions included in the module to expose an MCP WebSocket or SSE endpoint with a few lines of code.

The MCP Kotlin SDK Core module provides fundamental functionality for interacting with Model Context Protocol (MCP). This module serves as the foundation for building MCP-enabled applications in Kotlin.

[kotlin-sdk-server](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-server/index.html)

Link copied to clipboard

The server module provides core server-side functionality for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) Kotlin SDK. This module includes essential parts for writing MCP server applications.

---

# kotlin-sdk-client

kotlin-sdk-client
=================

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) Kotlin SDK client module provides a multiplatform implementation for connecting to and interacting with MCP servers.

Features
--------

*   Cross-platform support for JVM, WASM, JS, and Native
    
*   Built-in support for multiple transport protocols (stdio, SSE, WebSocket)
    
*   Type-safe API for resource management and server interactions
    

Packages
--------

[io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/index.html)

Link copied to clipboard

common

# mcpSse

mcpSse
======

suspend fun [HttpClient](https://api.ktor.io/ktor-client/ktor-client-core/io.ktor.client/-http-client/index.html)
.[mcpSse](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/mcp-sse.html)
(urlString: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
? = null, reconnectionTime: [Duration](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.time/-duration/index.html)
? = null, requestBuilder: [HttpRequestBuilder](https://api.ktor.io/ktor-client/ktor-client-core/io.ktor.client.request/-http-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
 = {}): [Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-client/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/client/KtorClient.kt#L32)
)

Creates and connects an MCP client over SSE using the provided HttpClient.

#### Return

A connected [Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/index.html)
 ready for MCP communication.

#### Parameters

urlString

Optional URL of the MCP server.

reconnectionTime

Optional duration to wait before attempting to reconnect.

requestBuilder

Optional lambda to configure the HTTP request.

# mcpClient

mcpClient
=========

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

suspend fun [mcpClient](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/mcp-client.html)
(clientInfo: [Implementation](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-implementation/index.html)
, clientOptions: [ClientOptions](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client-options/index.html)
 = ClientOptions(), transport: [Transport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-transport/index.html)
): [Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-client/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/client/Client.kt#L86)
)

Initializes and connects an MCP client using the provided clientInfo [Implementation](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-implementation/index.html)
, client options, and transport mechanism.

#### Return

An instance of [Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/index.html)
 that is connected and ready for use with the specified transport.

#### Parameters

clientInfo

The implementation details of the MCP client, including its name, version, and other metadata.

clientOptions

Optional client configuration settings, such as supported capabilities and strict enforcement options. Defaults to a new instance of ClientOptions.

transport

The transport mechanism used for communication.

mcpStreamableHttpTransport
==========================

fun [HttpClient](https://api.ktor.io/ktor-client/ktor-client-core/io.ktor.client/-http-client/index.html)
.[mcpStreamableHttpTransport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/mcp-streamable-http-transport.html)
(url: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
, reconnectionTime: [Duration](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.time/-duration/index.html)
? = null, requestBuilder: [HttpRequestBuilder](https://api.ktor.io/ktor-client/ktor-client-core/io.ktor.client.request/-http-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
 = {}): [StreamableHttpClientTransport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-streamable-http-client-transport/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-client/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/client/StreamableHttpMcpKtorClientExtensions.kt#L18)
)

Returns a new Streamable HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol using the provided HttpClient.

#### Return

A [StreamableHttpClientTransport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-streamable-http-client-transport/index.html)
 configured for MCP communication.

#### Parameters

url

URL of the MCP server.

reconnectionTime

Optional duration to wait before attempting to reconnect.

requestBuilder

Optional lambda to configure the HTTP request.

Creates and connects an MCP client over WebSocket using the provided HttpClient.

#### Return

A connected [Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/index.html)
 ready for MCP communication.

#### Parameters

urlString

Optional URL of the MCP server.

requestBuilder

Optional lambda to configure the HTTP request.

# StderrSeverity

StderrSeverity
==============

enum [StderrSeverity](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/index.html)
 : [Enum](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-enum/index.html)
<[StdioClientTransport.StderrSeverity](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/index.html)
\> ([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-client/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/client/StdioClientTransport.kt#L112)
)

Severity classification for stderr messages.

*   [FATAL](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/-f-a-t-a-l/index.html)
    : Calls error handler and terminates transport.
    
*   [WARNING](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/-w-a-r-n-i-n-g/index.html)
    : Logs at WARN level, transport continues.
    
*   [INFO](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/-i-n-f-o/index.html)
    : Logs at INFO level, transport continues.
    
*   [DEBUG](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/-d-e-b-u-g/index.html)
    : Logs at DEBUG level, transport continues.
    
*   [IGNORE](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/-i-g-n-o-r-e/index.html)
    : Discards message silently, transport continues.

# StdioClientTransport

StdioClientTransport
====================

class [StdioClientTransport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/index.html)
 @[JvmOverloads](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.jvm/-jvm-overloads/index.html) constructor(input: Source, output: Sink, error: Source? = null, sendChannel: [Channel](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines.channels/-channel/index.html)
<[JSONRPCMessage](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-j-s-o-n-r-p-c-message/index.html)
\> = Channel(Channel.BUFFERED), classifyStderr: ([String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
) -> [StdioClientTransport.StderrSeverity](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/index.html)
 = { DEBUG }) : [AbstractTransport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-abstract-transport/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-client/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/client/StdioClientTransport.kt#L85)
)

A transport implementation for JSON-RPC communication over standard I/O streams.

Reads JSON-RPC messages from [input](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/--root--.html)
 and writes messages to [output](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/--root--.html)
. Optionally monitors [error](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/--root--.html)
 stream for stderr output with configurable severity handling.

Usage Example
-------------

    val process = ProcessBuilder("mcp-server").start()val transport = StdioClientTransport(    input = process.inputStream.asSource().buffered(),    output = process.outputStream.asSink().buffered(),    error = process.errorStream.asSource().buffered()) { stderrLine ->    when {        stderrLine.contains("error", ignoreCase = true) -> StderrSeverity.FATAL        stderrLine.contains("warning", ignoreCase = true) -> StderrSeverity.WARNING        else -> StderrSeverity.INFO    }}transport.start()

Content copied to clipboard

#### Parameters

input

The input stream where messages are received.

output

The output stream where messages are sent.

error

Optional error stream for stderr monitoring.

sendChannel

Channel for outbound messages. Default: buffered channel (implementation-default capacity).

classifyStderr

Callback to classify stderr lines. Return [StderrSeverity.FATAL](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-stdio-client-transport/-stderr-severity/-f-a-t-a-l/index.html)
 to fail transport, or StderrSeverity.WARNING / StderrSeverity.INFO / StderrSeverity.DEBUG to log, or StderrSeverity.IGNORE to discard. Default value: StderrSeverity.DEBUG.

#### See also

[MCP Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/transports#stdio)

[Channel.Factory.BUFFERED](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines.channels/-channel/-factory/-b-u-f-f-e-r-e-d.html)

# StreamableHttpClientTransport

StreamableHttpClientTransport
=============================

class [StreamableHttpClientTransport](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-streamable-http-client-transport/index.html)
(client: [HttpClient](https://api.ktor.io/ktor-client/ktor-client-core/io.ktor.client/-http-client/index.html)
, url: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)

? = null, requestBuilder: [HttpRequestBuilder](https://api.ktor.io/ktor-client/ktor-client-core/io.ktor.client.request/-http-request-builder/index.html)

)

Client transport for Streamable HTTP: this implements the MCP Streamable HTTP transport specification. It will connect to a server using HTTP POST for sending messages and HTTP GET with Server-Sent Events for receiving messages.

# kotlin-sdk-core

kotlin-sdk-core
===============

commonjsjvmnativewasmJs

The MCP Kotlin SDK Core module provides fundamental functionality for interacting with Model Context Protocol (MCP). This module serves as the foundation for building MCP-enabled applications in Kotlin.

# Client

Client
======

open class [Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/index.html)
(clientInfo: [Implementation](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-implementation/index.html)
, options: [ClientOptions](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client-options/index.html)
 = ClientOptions()) : [Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-protocol/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-client/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/client/Client.kt#L110)
)

An MCP client on top of a pluggable transport.

The client automatically performs the initialization handshake with the server when [connect](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/connect.html)
 is called. After initialization, [serverCapabilities](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/server-capabilities.html)
 and [serverVersion](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/server-version.html)
 provide details about the connected server.

You can extend this class with custom request/notification/result types if needed.

#### Parameters

clientInfo

Information about the client implementation (name, version).

options

Configuration options for this client.

Members

Constructors
------------

[Client](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-client/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.client/-client/-client.html)

### Deprecated

Use \`CancelledNotification\` instead

#### Replace with

    import io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types.CancelledNotification

Content copied to clipboard

    CancelledNotification

Content copied to clipboard

This notification can be sent by either side to indicate that it is cancelling a previously issued request.

The request SHOULD still be in-flight, but due to communication latency, it is always possible that this notification MAY arrive after the request has already finished.

This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.

A client MUST NOT attempt to cancel its `initialize` request.

---

Content copied to clipboard

    CreateMessageRequest

Content copied to clipboard

A request from the server to sample an LLM via the client. The client has full discretion over which model to select. The client should also inform the user before beginning sampling to allow them to inspect the request (human in the loop) and decide whether to approve it.

The client's response to a sampling/create\_message request from the server. The client should inform the user before returning the sampled message to allow them to inspect the response (human in the loop) and decide whether to allow the server to see it.

fun [~CreateMessageRequest~](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-create-message-request.html)
(params: [CreateMessageRequestParams](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-params/index.html)
): [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)

A request from the server to sample an LLM via the client. The client has full discretion over which model to select. The client should also inform the user before beginning sampling to allow them to inspect the request (human in the loop) and decide whether to approve it.

# ExperimentalMcpApi

ExperimentalMcpApi
==================

@[Target](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-target/index.html)
(allowedTargets = \[[AnnotationTarget.CLASS](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-c-l-a-s-s/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.ANNOTATION\_CLASS](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-a-n-n-o-t-a-t-i-o-n_-c-l-a-s-s/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-p-r-o-p-e-r-t-y/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.FIELD](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-f-i-e-l-d/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.LOCAL\_VARIABLE](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-l-o-c-a-l_-v-a-r-i-a-b-l-e/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.VALUE\_PARAMETER](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-v-a-l-u-e_-p-a-r-a-m-e-t-e-r/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.CONSTRUCTOR](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-c-o-n-s-t-r-u-c-t-o-r/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.FUNCTION](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-f-u-n-c-t-i-o-n/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY\_GETTER](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-p-r-o-p-e-r-t-y_-g-e-t-t-e-r/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY\_SETTER](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-p-r-o-p-e-r-t-y_-s-e-t-t-e-r/index.html)\
, [AnnotationTarget.TYPEALIAS](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.annotation/-annotation-target/-t-y-p-e-a-l-i-a-s/index.html)\
\])

annotation class [ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/ExperimentalMcpApi.kt#L57)
)

Annotation marking an API as experimental and subject to changes or removal in the future.

This annotation is used to signal that a particular element, such as a class, function, or property, is part of an experimental API. Such APIs may not be stable, and their usage requires opting in explicitly.

Users of the annotated API must explicitly accept the opt-in requirement to ensure they are aware of the potential instability or unfinished nature of the API.

Targets that can be annotated include:

*   Classes
    
*   Annotation classes
    
*   Properties
    
*   Fields
    
*   Local variables
    
*   Value parameters
    
*   Constructors
    
*   Functions
    
*   Property getters
    
*   Property setters
    
*   Type aliases

Content copied to clipboard

    ToolAnnotations

Content copied to clipboard

Additional properties describing a Tool to clients.

NOTE: all properties in ToolAnnotations are **hints**. They are not guaranteed to provide a faithful description of tool behavior (including descriptive properties like `title`).

Clients should never make tool use decisions based on ToolAnnotations received from untrusted servers.

---

# Protocol

Protocol
========

abstract class [Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-protocol/index.html)
(options: [ProtocolOptions](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-protocol-options/index.html)
?)([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/shared/Protocol.kt#L161)
)

Implements MCP protocol framing on top of a pluggable transport, including features like request/response linking, notifications, and progress.

[onProgress](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-request-options/on-progress.html)

Link copied to clipboard

val [onProgress](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-request-options/on-progress.html)
: [ProgressCallback](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-progress-callback/index.html)
?

callback for progress notifications. If set, requests progress notifications from the remote end (if supported). When progress notifications are received, this callback will be invoked.

[onResumptionToken](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-transport-send-options/on-resumption-token.html)

Link copied to clipboard

val [onResumptionToken](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-transport-send-options/on-resumption-token.html)
: ([String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
) -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
?

a callback that is invoked when the resumption token changes, if supported by the transport. This allows clients to persist the latest token for potential reconnection.

[relatedRequestId](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-transport-send-options/related-request-id.html)

?

if present, `relatedRequestId` is used to indicate to the transport which incoming request to associate this outgoing message with.

: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
?

the resumption token used to continue long-running requests that were interrupted. This allows clients to reconnect and continue from where they left off, if supported by the transport.

[timeout](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-request-options/timeout.html)

Link copied to clipboard

val [timeout](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.shared/-request-options/timeout.html)
: [Duration](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.time/-duration/index.html)

a timeout for this request. If exceeded, a McpException with code `RequestTimeout` will be raised from request(). If not specified, `DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT` will be used as the timeout.

a callback that is invoked when the resumption token changes, if supported by the transport. This allows clients to persist the latest token for potential reconnection.

?

[audience](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-annotations/audience.html)

Link copied to clipboard

val [audience](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-annotations/audience.html)
: [List](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.collections/-list/index.html)
<[Role](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-role/index.html)
\>?

Describes who the intended customer of this object or data is. Can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences (e.g., Role.user, Role.assistant).

[lastModified](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-annotations/last-modified.html)

Link copied to clipboard

val [lastModified](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-annotations/last-modified.html)
: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
?

The moment the resource was last modified, as an ISO 8601 formatted string (e.g., "2025-01-12T15:00:58Z"). Examples: last activity timestamp in an open file, timestamp when the resource was attached, etc.

[priority](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-annotations/priority.html)

Link copied to clipboard

val [priority](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-annotations/priority.html)
: [Double](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-double/index.html)
?

Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of 1.0 means "most important" and indicates that the data is effectively required, while 0.0 means "least important" and indicates that the data is entirely optional. Should be a value between 0.0 and 1.0.

Example:

    messages {    assistantText {        text = "Here's the answer"        annotations(priority = 0.8)    }}

# buildCallToolRequest

buildCallToolRequest
====================

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

inline fun [buildCallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-call-tool-request.html)
(block: [CallToolRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
): [CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/tools.dsl.kt#L46)
)

Creates a [CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)
 using a type-safe DSL builder.

Required
--------

*   [name](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/name.html)
     - The name of the tool to call
    

Optional
--------

*   [arguments](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/arguments.html)
     - Arguments to pass to the tool
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Metadata for the request
    

Example without arguments:

    val request = buildCallToolRequest {    name = "getCurrentTime"}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with arguments:

    val request = buildCallToolRequest {    name = "searchDatabase"    arguments {        put("query", "users")        put("limit", 10)    }}

Content copied to clipboard

#### Return

A configured [CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)
 instance

#### Parameters

block

Configuration lambda for setting up the call tool request

#### See also

[CallToolRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/index.html)

[CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)

---

# AudioContentBuilder

AudioContentBuilder
===================

class [AudioContentBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-audio-content-builder/index.html)
 : [MediaContentBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/content.dsl.kt#L246)
)

DSL builder for constructing [AudioContent](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-audio-content/index.html)
 instances.

Required
--------

*   [data](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-audio-content-builder/data.html)
     - The base64-encoded audio data
    
*   [mimeType](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-audio-content-builder/mime-type.html)
     - The MIME type of the audio (e.g., "audio/wav", "audio/mpeg")
    

Optional
--------

*   [annotations](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content-builder/annotations.html)
     - Content annotations (inherited from [MediaContentBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content-builder/index.html)
    )
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content-builder/meta.html)
     - Additional metadata (inherited from [MediaContentBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content-builder/index.html)
    )
    

Example usage in sampling messages:

    createMessageRequest {    maxTokens = 100    messages {        userAudio {            data = "UklGRiQAAABXQVZFZm10IBAAAAABAAEA..."            mimeType = "audio/wav"        }    }}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with annotations:

    userAudio {    data = base64EncodedAudioData    mimeType = "audio/mpeg"    annotations(Annotations(priority = 0.7))}

Content copied to clipboard

#### See also

[AudioContent](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-audio-content/index.html)

[MediaContentBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content-builder/index.html)

Members

----------

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

inline fun [buildElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-elicit-request.html)
(block: [ElicitRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
): [ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/elicitation.dsl.kt#L62)
)

Creates an [ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)
 using a type-safe DSL builder.

Required
--------

*   [message](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/message.html)
     - The message to present to the user
    
*   [requestedSchema](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/requested-schema.html)
     - Schema defining the structure of requested data
    

Optional
--------

*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Metadata for the request
    

Example requesting user information:

    val request = buildElicitRequest {    message = "Please provide your contact information"    requestedSchema {        properties {            put("email", JsonObject(mapOf(                "type" to JsonPrimitive("string"),                "description" to JsonPrimitive("Your email address")            )))            put("name", JsonObject(mapOf(                "type" to JsonPrimitive("string")            )))        }        required = listOf("email")    }}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with simple text input:

    val request = buildElicitRequest {    message = "Enter a project name"    requestedSchema {        properties {            put("projectName", JsonObject(mapOf(                "type" to JsonPrimitive("string"),                "description" to JsonPrimitive("Name for the new project")            )))        }    }}

Content copied to clipboard

#### Return

A configured [ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)
 instance

#### Parameters

block

Configuration lambda for setting up the elicitation request

#### See also

[ElicitRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/index.html)

[ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)

# buildCompleteRequest

buildCompleteRequest
====================

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

inline fun [buildCompleteRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-complete-request.html)
(block: [CompleteRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
): [CompleteRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/completion.dsl.kt#L45)
)

Creates a [CompleteRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request/index.html)
 using a type-safe DSL builder.

Required
--------

*   [argument](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/argument.html)
     - Sets the argument name and value to complete
    
*   [ref](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/ref.html)
     - Sets the reference to a prompt or resource template
    

Optional
--------

*   [context](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/context.html)
     - Adds additional context for the completion
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Adds metadata to the request
    

Example with [PromptReference](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-prompt-reference/index.html)
:

    val request = buildCompleteRequest {    argument("query", "user input")    ref(PromptReference("searchPrompt"))}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with [ResourceTemplateReference](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-resource-template-reference/index.html)
:

    val request = buildCompleteRequest {    argument("path", "/users/123")    ref(ResourceTemplateReference("file:///{path}"))    context {        put("userId", "123")        put("role", "admin")    }}

Content copied to clipboard

#### Return

A configured [CompleteRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request/index.html)
 instance

#### Parameters

block

Configuration lambda for setting up the completion request

#### See also

[CompleteRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/index.html)

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

inline fun [buildGetPromptRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-get-prompt-request.html)
(block: [GetPromptRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-get-prompt-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
): [GetPromptRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-get-prompt-request/index.html)

)

 using a type-safe DSL builder.

Required
--------

*   [name](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-get-prompt-request-builder/name.html)
     - The name of the prompt to retrieve
    

Optional
--------

     - Arguments to pass to the prompt
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Metadata for the request
    

Example without arguments:

    val request = buildGetPromptRequest {    name = "greeting"}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with arguments:

    val request = buildGetPromptRequest {    name = "userProfile"    arguments = mapOf(        "userId" to "123",        "includeDetails" to "true"    )}

Content copied to clipboard

# buildCreateMessageRequest

buildCreateMessageRequest
=========================

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

inline fun [buildCreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-create-message-request.html)
(block: [CreateMessageRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
): [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/sampling.dsl.kt#L62)
)

Creates a [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)
 using a type-safe DSL builder.

Required
--------

*   [maxTokens](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/max-tokens.html)
     - Maximum number of tokens to generate
    
*   [messages](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/messages.html)
     - List of conversation messages
    

Optional
--------

*   [systemPrompt](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/system-prompt.html)
     - System-level instructions
    
*   [context](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/context.html)
     - Context inclusion settings
    
*   [temperature](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/temperature.html)
     - Sampling temperature
    
*   [stopSequences](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/stop-sequences.html)
     - Sequences that stop generation
    
*   [preferences](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/preferences.html)
     - Model selection preferences
    
*   [metadata](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/metadata.html)
     - Additional metadata
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Request metadata
    

Example:

    val request = buildCreateMessageRequest {    maxTokens = 1000    systemPrompt = "You are a helpful assistant"    messages {        user { "What is the capital of France?" }        assistant { "The capital of France is Paris." }        user { "What about Germany?" }    }}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with preferences:

    val request = buildCreateMessageRequest {    maxTokens = 500    temperature = 0.7    preferences(        hints = listOf("claude-3-sonnet"),        intelligence = 0.8    )    messages {        user { "Explain quantum computing" }    }}

Content copied to clipboard

#### Return

A configured [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)
 instance

#### Parameters

block

Configuration lambda for setting up the create message request

#### See also

[CreateMessageRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/index.html)

# buildInitializeRequest

buildInitializeRequest
======================

@[ExperimentalMcpApi](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk/-experimental-mcp-api/index.html)

inline fun [buildInitializeRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-initialize-request.html)
(block: [InitializeRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request-builder/index.html)
.() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)
): [InitializeRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/initialize.dsl.kt#L57)
)

Creates an [InitializeRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request/index.html)
 using a type-safe DSL builder.

Required
--------

*   [protocolVersion](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request-builder/protocol-version.html)
     - MCP protocol version
    
*   [capabilities](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request-builder/capabilities.html)
     - Client capabilities
    
*   [info](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request-builder/info.html)
     - Client implementation information
    

Optional
--------

*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Metadata for the request
    

Example:

    val request = buildInitializeRequest {    protocolVersion = "2024-11-05"    capabilities {        sampling(ClientCapabilities.sampling)        roots(listChanged = true)    }    info("MyClient", "1.0.0")}

Content copied to clipboard

Example with full client info:

    val request = buildInitializeRequest {    protocolVersion = "2024-11-05"    capabilities {        sampling(ClientCapabilities.sampling)        experimental {            put("feature", JsonPrimitive(true))        }    }    info(        name = "MyAdvancedClient",        version = "2.0.0",        title = "Advanced MCP Client",        websiteUrl = "https://example.com"    )}

Content copied to clipboard

#### Return

A configured [InitializeRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request/index.html)
 instance

#### Parameters

block

Configuration lambda for setting up the initialize request

#### See also

[InitializeRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request-builder/index.html)

[InitializeRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request/index.html)

# CallToolRequestBuilder

CallToolRequestBuilder
======================

class [CallToolRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/index.html)
 : [RequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/tools.dsl.kt#L67)
)

DSL builder for constructing [CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)
 instances.

This builder invokes a specific tool by name with optional arguments.

Required
--------

*   [name](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/name.html)
     - The name of the tool to call
    

Optional
--------

*   [arguments](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-builder/arguments.html)
     - Arguments to pass to the tool
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Metadata for the request
    

#### See also

# Roots

Roots
=====

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [Roots](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities/-roots/index.html)
(val listChanged: [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html)
? = null)([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/capabilities.kt#L73)
)

Indicates that the client supports listing roots.

Roots are the top-level directories or locations that the server can access. When present (non-null), the server can query available roots and optionally receive notifications when the roots list changes.

?

Whether the client supports notifications for changes to the roots list. If true, the client will send notifications when roots are added or removed.

# CallToolResult

CallToolResult
==============

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [CallToolResult](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-result/index.html)
(val content: [List](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.collections/-list/index.html)
<[ContentBlock](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-content-block/index.html)
\>, val isError: [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html)
? = null, val structuredContent: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
? = null, val meta: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
? = null) : [ServerResult](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-server-result/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/tools.kt#L203)
)

The server's response to a [CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)
.

Contains the result of the tool execution, which can be successful or an error.

**Important error handling:**

*   Errors that originate from the **tool itself** SHOULD be reported inside the result object with [isError](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-result/--root--.html)
     set to true, NOT as an MCP protocol-level error response. This allows the LLM to see that an error occurred and potentially self-correct.
    
*   Errors in **finding the tool**, unsupported operations, or other exceptional conditions SHOULD be reported as MCP protocol-level error responses.

# ClientCapabilities

ClientCapabilities
==================

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [ClientCapabilities](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities/index.html)
(val sampling: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
? = null, val roots: [ClientCapabilities.Roots](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities/-roots/index.html)
? = null, val elicitation: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
? = null, val experimental: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
? = null)([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/capabilities.kt#L50)
)

Capabilities that a client may support.

Known capabilities are defined here, but this is not a closed set: any client can define its own additional capabilities through the [experimental](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities/--root--.html)
 field.

The presence of a capability object (non-null value) indicates that the client supports that capability.

# CallToolRequest

CallToolRequest
===============

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [CallToolRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request/index.html)
(val params: [CallToolRequestParams](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-call-tool-request-params/index.html)
) : [ClientRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/tools.kt#L140)
)

Used by the client to invoke a tool provided by the server.

Tools allow servers to expose functionality that clients (and LLMs) can call. Examples include file operations, API calls, database queries, or any other server-side operations.

# ClientCapabilitiesBuilder

ClientCapabilitiesBuilder
=========================

class [ClientCapabilitiesBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/capabilities.dsl.kt#L38)
)

DSL builder for constructing [ClientCapabilities](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities/index.html)
 instances.

This builder is used within [InitializeRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-initialize-request-builder/index.html)
 to configure client capabilities. All capabilities are optional - the presence of a capability indicates support for that feature.

Available Functions (all optional)
----------------------------------

*   [sampling](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities-builder/sampling.html)
     - Indicates support for sampling from an LLM
    
*   [roots](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities-builder/roots.html)
     - Indicates support for listing roots
    
*   [elicitation](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities-builder/elicitation.html)
     - Indicates support for elicitation from the server
    
*   [experimental](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-capabilities-builder/experimental.html)
     - Defines experimental, non-standard capabilities
    

Example usage within [buildInitializeRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-initialize-request.html)
:

    val request = buildInitializeRequest {    protocolVersion = "1.0"    capabilities {        sampling(ClientCapabilities.sampling)        roots(listChanged = true)        experimental {            put("customFeature", JsonPrimitive(true))        }    }    info("MyClient", "1.0.0")}

# CancelledNotification

CancelledNotification
=====================

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [CancelledNotification](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-cancelled-notification/index.html)
(val params: [CancelledNotificationParams](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-cancelled-notification-params/index.html)
) : [ClientNotification](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-notification/index.html)
, [ServerNotification](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-server-notification/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/notification.kt#L103)
)

This notification can be sent by either side to indicate that it is cancelling a previously-issued request.

The request SHOULD still be in-flight, but due to communication latency, it is always possible that this notification MAY arrive after the request has already finished.

This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.

A client MUST NOT attempt to cancel its `initialize` request.

# CompleteRequestBuilder

CompleteRequestBuilder
======================

class [CompleteRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/index.html)
 : [RequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/completion.dsl.kt#L59)
)

DSL builder for constructing [CompleteRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request/index.html)
 instances.

This builder provides methods to configure completion requests for prompts or resource templates. Both [argument](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/argument.html)
 and [ref](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/ref.html)
 are required; [context](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-complete-request-builder/context.html)
 is optional.

#### See also

[buildCompleteRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-complete-request.html)

Indicates whether there are additional completion options beyond those provided in the current response, even if the exact total is unknown. Use this when the complete set of completions is too large to calculate upfront.

: [Int](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-int/index.html)
?

The total number of completion options available. This can exceed the number of values actually sent in the response, indicating that pagination or filtering may be needed.

# CreateMessageResult

CreateMessageResult
===================

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [CreateMessageResult](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-result/index.html)
(val role: [Role](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-role/index.html)
, val content: [MediaContent](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-media-content/index.html)
, val model: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
, val stopReason: [StopReason](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-stop-reason/index.html)
? = null, val meta: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
? = null) : [ClientResult](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-result/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/sampling.kt#L237)
)

The client's response to a [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)
 from the server.

The client should inform the user before returning the sampled message, to allow them to inspect the response (human in the loop) and decide whether to allow the server to see it.

# CreateMessageRequestBuilder

CreateMessageRequestBuilder
===========================

class [CreateMessageRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/index.html)
 : [RequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/sampling.dsl.kt#L89)
)

DSL builder for constructing [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)
 instances.

This builder creates LLM sampling requests with conversation history.

Required
--------

*   [maxTokens](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/max-tokens.html)
     - Maximum number of tokens to generate
    
*   [messages](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/messages.html)
     - List of conversation messages
    

Optional
--------

*   [systemPrompt](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/system-prompt.html)
     - System-level instructions
    
*   [context](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/context.html)
     - Context inclusion settings
    
*   [temperature](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/temperature.html)
     - Sampling temperature (0.0-1.0)
    
*   [stopSequences](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/stop-sequences.html)
     - Sequences that stop generation
    
*   [preferences](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/preferences.html)
     - Model selection preferences
    
*   [metadata](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-builder/metadata.html)
     - Additional metadata
    
*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Request metadata
    

#### See also

# CreateMessageRequest

CreateMessageRequest
====================

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [CreateMessageRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request/index.html)
(val params: [CreateMessageRequestParams](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-create-message-request-params/index.html)
) : [ServerRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-server-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/sampling.kt#L106)
)

A request from the server to sample an LLM via the client.

The client has full discretion over which model to select. The client should also inform the user before beginning sampling, to allow them to inspect the request (human in the loop) and decide whether to approve it.

**Important:** This is a request from **server to client**, not client to server. The server is asking the client to use its LLM to generate a completion.

**Human-in-the-loop:** Clients should:

1.  Show the sampling request to the user before executing it
    
2.  Allow the user to approve or reject the request
    
3.  Show the generated response to the user before sending it back to the server

# CustomNotification

CustomNotification
==================

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [CustomNotification](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-custom-notification/index.html)
(val method: [Method](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-method/index.html)
, val params: [BaseNotificationParams](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-base-notification-params/index.html)
? = null) : [ClientNotification](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-client-notification/index.html)
, [ServerNotification](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-server-notification/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/notification.kt#L78)
)

Represents a custom notification method that is not part of the core MCP specification.

The MCP protocol allows implementations to define custom methods for extending functionality. This class captures such custom notifications while preserving all their data.

# ElicitRequestedSchemaBuilder

ElicitRequestedSchemaBuilder
============================

class [ElicitRequestedSchemaBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-requested-schema-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/elicitation.dsl.kt#L188)
)

DSL builder for constructing [ElicitRequestParams.RequestedSchema](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-params/-requested-schema/index.html)
 instances.

Defines the JSON Schema structure for data to be collected from the user. Only supports top-level primitive properties.

Required
--------

*   [properties](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-requested-schema-builder/properties.html)
     - Schema definitions for each field
    

Optional
--------

*   [required](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-requested-schema-builder/required.html)
     - List of required property names
    

Example:

    requestedSchema {    properties {        put("username", JsonObject(mapOf(            "type" to JsonPrimitive("string"),            "description" to JsonPrimitive("Your username")        )))    }    required = listOf("username")}

Content copied to clipboard

#### See also

[ElicitRequestParams.RequestedSchema](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-params/-requested-schema/index.html)

Members

----------

<[String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
\>?

(val properties: [JsonObject](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-json/kotlinx.serialization.json/-json-object/index.html)
, val required: [List](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin.collections/-list/index.html)
<[String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)
\>? = null)([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/elicitation.kt#L75)
)

A restricted JSON Schema for elicitation requests.

Only supports top-level primitive properties without nesting. Each property represents a field in the form or dialog presented to the user.

# ElicitRequestBuilder

ElicitRequestBuilder
====================

class [ElicitRequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/index.html)
 : [RequestBuilder](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/elicitation.dsl.kt#L84)
)

DSL builder for constructing [ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)
 instances.

This builder is used to create requests that prompt users for structured input through forms or dialogs presented by the client.

Required
--------

*   [message](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/message.html)
     - The message to present to the user explaining what is being requested
    
*   [requestedSchema](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-builder/requested-schema.html)
     - Schema defining the structure of the data to collect
    

Optional
--------

*   [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-request-builder/meta.html)
     - Metadata for the request
    

#### See also

[buildElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/build-elicit-request.html)

[ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)

 and [meta](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/error.html)
, with `isError` being true.

# ElicitRequest

ElicitRequest
=============

@[Serializable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.serialization/kotlinx-serialization-core/kotlinx.serialization/-serializable/index.html)

data class [ElicitRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request/index.html)
(val params: [ElicitRequestParams](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-elicit-request-params/index.html)
) : [ServerRequest](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/kotlin-sdk/kotlin-sdk-core/io.modelcontextprotocol.kotlin.sdk.types/-server-request/index.html)
([source](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin-sdk/tree/main/kotlin-sdk-core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/modelcontextprotocol/kotlin/sdk/types/elicitation.kt#L19)
)

A request from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

This request type allows servers to prompt users for structured input through forms or dialogs presented by the client. The server defines a schema for the requested data, and the client presents an appropriate UI to collect this information.

MembersMembers & Extensions
